name: Journal of Scientific Exploration
description: The flagship peer-reviewed journal of the Society for Scientific Exploration, publishing research on topics at the frontier of science including UAP, consciousness, parapsychology, and anomalous phenomena. One of the most citable journals in anomalistics, with contributions from credentialed researchers across physics, medicine, psychology, and the social sciences. Several papers directly cited in DECUR's research hub appear in JSE, including the Vallee/Davis incommensurability paper.

name: Limina: Journal of UAP Studies
description: The first dedicated peer-reviewed academic journal focused exclusively on UAP studies, launched to fill the gap in legitimate academic publishing infrastructure for UAP research. Publishes work spanning empirical data analysis, political science, philosophy, social science, and interdisciplinary approaches to UAP phenomena. Multiple Phase 2 and Phase 4 papers in DECUR's research hub were published in Limina, including work by Powell, Bohlander, Ouellet, Denzler, Bower, and Kelly.

name: Journal of Near-Death Studies
description: The primary peer-reviewed journal for NDE research, published by IANDS since 1981. Covers clinical studies of near-death experiences, retrospective analysis of NDE accounts, cross-cultural comparisons, and the theoretical implications of NDE phenomenology for consciousness science. Research from DOPS (UVA), Parnia Labs (NYU), and Jeffrey Long's NDERF database frequently appears in JNDS.

name: Journal of Parapsychology
description: The longest-running peer-reviewed journal in parapsychology, founded in 1937 by J.B. Rhine at Duke University. Publishes experimental studies of ESP, psychokinesis, and anomalous cognition under controlled conditions, using statistical methodology consistent with mainstream psychological research. The PA's AAAS affiliation distinguishes this journal from popular parapsychology literature. Rhine Research Center archives dating to the journal's founding make it an indispensable historical record.

name: Journal of Consciousness Studies
description: A leading interdisciplinary journal bridging philosophy of mind, neuroscience, cognitive science, and related fields in the study of consciousness. JCS publishes debates, target articles with peer commentary, and original research on the hard problem of consciousness, quantum approaches to mind, and non-ordinary states of awareness. Directly relevant to DECUR's consciousness-research thread and the post-materialist science community represented by SSE, AAPS, and SMN.

name: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
description: The peer-reviewed journal of the Society for Psychical Research, one of the oldest scientific periodicals on anomalous cognition and psychical phenomena. Published since 1884, JSPR is an essential historical record of psychical research from the Cambridge scientists who founded SPR through to contemporary parapsychology. Contains early case studies that predate modern UAP terminology but document anomalous phenomena with rigorous Victorian-era empirical methods.

name: Australian Journal of Parapsychology
description: The peer-reviewed journal of the Australian Institute of Parapsychological Research, covering empirical and theoretical research on psi phenomena from the Asia-Pacific research community. Provides geographic diversity to the parapsychology publication landscape dominated by US and UK journals, and publishes work from researchers across Australasia and Southeast Asia.

name: Journal of Anomalistics
description: The peer-reviewed journal of the German Society for Anomalistics (Gesellschaft fur Anomalistik), publishing German-language and English-language research on the full spectrum of anomalous phenomena. Applies a dual-methodological approach: examining both the phenomena themselves and the psychological and social factors that shape anomalous belief. A significant node in European anomalistics research alongside the JSE and JSPR.

name: Journal of UFO Studies
description: The peer-reviewed journal of the Center for UFO Studies, founded by J. Allen Hynek, the original scientific consultant to Project Blue Book. JUFOS published empirical UFO case analysis, methodology papers, and theoretical contributions from the 1970s through the early 2000s. Though no longer actively publishing new issues, the JUFOS archive is an essential historical record of systematic scientific UFO investigation in the pre-UAP era.

name: Paranthropology
description: An independent open-access journal taking an anthropological approach to paranormal and anomalous experience. Paranthropology examines anomalous phenomena through the lens of cultural anthropology, comparative religion, and the social sciences, treating experiencer accounts as anthropologically significant data rather than either credulous acceptance or reflexive debunking. Particularly relevant to the experiencer research thread in DECUR, including coverage of UAP contact, NDE, and related phenomena.

name: Explore: Journal of Science and Healing
description: An Elsevier journal bridging conventional and integrative medicine, with a focus on consciousness, healing, and the scientific investigation of non-ordinary phenomena. Publishes peer-reviewed work on topics including energy medicine, consciousness-based healing, psi phenomena in clinical contexts, and the scientific study of spiritual experience. Relevant to DECUR's consciousness and post-materialist research threads.

name: International Journal of Astrobiology
description: A Cambridge University Press journal covering astrobiology, SETI, planetary habitability, and the implications of extraterrestrial life for science and society. The Cirkovic/Katic paper on ETI moral standing in DECUR's research hub was published here. Increasingly relevant as the mainstream scientific community engages with ETI implications adjacent to UAP research discussions.

name: Acta Astronautica
description: The peer-reviewed journal of the International Astronautical Federation covering aerospace science, propulsion, spacecraft systems, and space exploration. Relevant to DECUR's propulsion and advanced aerospace research thread, where peer-reviewed aerospace engineering provides contextual baseline for evaluating exotic propulsion claims in UAP reports.

name: Progress in Aerospace Sciences
description: An Elsevier review journal publishing comprehensive surveys of progress in aerospace engineering and aeronautics. Provides peer-reviewed technical grounding for evaluating propulsion, aerodynamics, and flight performance claims in UAP research contexts.

name: Zygon: Journal of Religion and Science
publisher: Institute on Religion in an Age of Science / Wiley-Blackwell
description: The leading peer-reviewed journal for the religion-science dialogue, covering consciousness, meaning-making, and the intersection of scientific and religious frameworks for understanding reality. The Agrama 2021 paper on synchronicity and uncanny science in DECUR's research hub was published here. Relevant to DECUR's coverage of how anomalous phenomena intersect with worldview and meaning.

name: MUFON Journal
description: The monthly publication of MUFON covering field investigation reports, case analyses, and UAP research news. While not a peer-reviewed academic journal in the traditional sense, the MUFON Journal constitutes a substantial ongoing record of civilian UAP investigation dating to 1967, with a large historical archive of case reports. MUFON has a dedicated program page on DECUR at /programs/mufon.

name: Aperture
description: The journal of the International Remote Viewing Association, covering remote viewing research, methodology, protocol development, and the history of the SRI/STARGATE program. Aperture is the primary publication venue for CRV protocol research and practitioner reports within the IRVA network, providing technical and historical documentation of the remote viewing research thread that runs through DECUR's coverage of Hal Puthoff, the Monroe Institute, and IRVA.
